reasoning:
The TGA balance on May 21, 2025, was approx $475B. The debt limit was reinstated on Jan 1, 2025, so the Treasury is largely constrained from issuing new debt and must draw down its balance (the TGA) as "extraordinary measures" until Congress acts to raise the ceiling. The X-date (when extraordinary measures run out) is projected between mid-July and October, with the Treasury Secretary recently warning of an August deadline and urging action by mid-July before August recess.

Since the base value on May 21 is $475B, we can expect the balance to continue bleeding down through June 1. June 1 gets a very low probability (20%) of being higher. June 24 gets a bump due to the large mid-June corporate tax inflows which notoriously cause the TGA to spike (often by $100B+), so the probability is around 60%. By August 23, either the debt limit is raised entirely before the August recess, allowing the Treasury to immediately issue bonds and massively replenish the TGA back toward its $850B target, or we are post-tax revenues but right at the X-date (which is less likely than a prior deal, given warnings, but would mean a TGA near $0). Assuming Congress strikes a deal by August to avert default, the TGA will rapidly shoot past $475B ($850B target). Hence an 85% probability for Aug 23. By November 21, the new fiscal year will have started, the debt limit issue will almost certainly have been resolved, and the TGA will comfortably reflect the new normal of $700B-$850B run rates, so 90%.
